_meta.standard: Weft Stable Entity Identity (SEI) conformance standard §8
_meta.description: The six shared SEI conformance scenarios every Weft tool runs against a reference Loomweave. Asserts BEHAVIOUR and OPACITY, never the SEI's internal form. A subsystem is SEI-conformant only when it passes all six (no grandfathering).

invariants: SEI is opaque: a consumer never parses it. It carries the reserved `loomweave:eid:` prefix and is NOT a locator.
Fail-closed: when sameness cannot be PROVEN, mint a new SEI and orphan the old one — never silently re-point.
Lineage is append-only: born / locator_changed / moved / orphaned / superseded.

Identity is carried (never re-minted) for an unchanged locator; SEI values are not part of the byte-identical-run guarantee, but carry/mint decisions are deterministic given the same bindings + source.

scenarios.given: A function entity is analyzed for the first time.
scenarios.when: Mint an SEI; resolve(locator) → sei; resolve_sei(sei) → locator.

expect.opacity: the returned `sei` begins with `loomweave:eid:` and is treated as an opaque string by the consumer (never parsed); it is not equal to the locator

scenarios.given: An entity with an alive SEI; its file/module is renamed so the locator prefix changes; the body is byte-identical; a git-rename signal maps old_locator → new_locator.
An entity with an alive SEI is moved to a new module; body hash AND signature are identical at the new locator; exactly one vanished candidate matches; no git signal required.
An entity is renamed WITH a body edit (the body hash changes), even if a git-rename signal is present.

expect.old_binding: orphaned (resolve_sei → alive:false with an `orphaned` lineage event)
expect.rationale: the matcher cannot PROVE sameness → fail closed; a governance attestation on the old SEI is never silently carried across an unproven match

scenarios.given: An entity present in a prior run is absent from the current run and was not rematched by a rename/move.
A Loomweave instance that has not populated SEI (pre-SEI DB, or `_capabilities.sei.supported` false / absent).

expect.consumer: detects the absent capability and DEGRADES gracefully — keeps working on locators, no crash, honest 'identity unavailable'
